hero_summary: Ajam Murassa is a maqam name found in some modern Arabic guides to derived maqamat, and it is placed within the Ajam family, with a very close relation to Ajam Ashiran and one basic difference in the fourth degree inside the lower jins.
short_identity: A maqam with relatively little published theoretical explanation, understood in practice more as an inlaid form of Ajam Ashiran than as a maqam structurally distant from it.

quick_facts.ghammaz: Emphasis extends toward the upper region according to treatment
quick_facts.lower_jins: Jins Ajam Murassa on Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat tonic)
quick_facts.character: Very close to Ajam Ashiran, with a murassa-type coloring on the fourth degree
quick_facts.common_start: It begins by stabilizing Ajam Ashiran, then revealing the inlaid difference on the fourth degree before completing the upper Ajam field

sayr.summary: Ajam Murassa is a maqam name found in some modern Arabic guides to derived maqamat, but it is not among the maqamat richly explained in the major theoretical literature in the way Rast, Huzam, or Shawq Afza are. So the more accurate way to present it is as a maqam with limited published theoretical explanation, not as a vague or non-existent maqam. According to the available teaching material, it belongs to the Ajam family and is presented as a maqam very close to Ajam Ashiran: it begins with Ajam Murassa on Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat tonic), and above it stands Ajam on Jaharkah (F), with one essential difference from Ajam Ashiran in the fourth degree, which is colored in a murassa way and changes the character of the lower jins.
resting_tones: Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat tonic): the tonic and primary center of attraction
The upper region takes its stability from the completion of Ajam on Jaharkah (F)
The special personality of the maqam appears through the coloring of the fourth degree inside the lower structure more than through any change in the tonic itself
motion_notes: The relationship to Ajam Ashiran is the most important point in understanding this maqam
It can be understood in practice as an inlaid form of Ajam Ashiran more than as a fully separate modal world
Its personality does not come from changing the tonic, but from an internal coloring inside the Ajam field
This is a structural inference supported by the available pedagogical description, not a claim from a fully detailed classical source
common_path: Stabilize Ajam Ashiran (low B-flat tonic) in the lower region
Bring out the special murassa character of the fourth degree
Complete the upper field through Ajam on Jaharkah (F)
Return while preserving the strong structural closeness to Ajam Ashiran

common_modulations: According to the available teaching material, Ajam Murassa belongs to the Ajam family
It is very close to Ajam Ashiran, to the point that the same pedagogical source says it is almost the same maqam except for the fourth degree

The existence of works titled Acem Murassa Peşrev in catalogues and manuscript/note sources shows that the name has historical repertoire presence, but that alone does not provide a full theoretical explanation of the structure

Scholarly caution is necessary here: there are signs that the name exists and a clear pedagogical structural description of it, but in this round there is no broad classical Turkish or Arabic reference literature explaining it independently in the way Ajam Ashiran or Shawq Afza are explained
